hypothesis: A synchronous public ylong_http_client path, aligned with the blocking libcurl easy baseline and with sync connector TCP_NODELAY plus sync HTTP/1 pool capacity preservation, may remove async runtime scheduling cost and produce SOTA-scale HTTPS proxy performance.

source_changes.summary: Adds a shared connect_tcp helper that sets TCP_NODELAY for sync TCP sockets before direct, HTTP-proxy, or HTTPS-proxy TLS use; a unit test verifies the socket option.
Completes linux+ylong_base+TLS ConnectorConfig construction for sync ClientBuilder by setting fchown: None, matching the conditional ConnectorConfig shape.
Preserves sync HTTP/1 dispatcher Vec capacity by scanning in place instead of replacing the Vec with std::mem::take; a regression test proves the previous capacity loss.

Adds an explicitly selected ylong_http_client_sync benchmark client that uses the public sync ClientBuilder, Proxy builder, Request::get, Client::request, and BodyReader drain path. Default all still selects the canonical async ylong client only.
Adds --ylong-client sync, parses ylong_http_client_sync output, and keeps sync candidate results separate from canonical async matrices.
Covers explicit sync candidate labeling, expected-client parsing, and benchmark client selection.

red.observed: failed before implementation: sync HTTP/1 list capacity dropped from 65 to 4 after reuse
failed before the socket-option test could run because sync ClientBuilder did not populate linux+ylong_base+TLS ConnectorConfig::fchown; this exposed an existing sync+TLS feature-gating build gap
failed before implementation because benchmark_clients did not accept ylong_client=sync

red.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_sync_bench_red cargo test -p ylong_http_client --bin https_proxy_bench ut_bench_clients_selects_sync_candidate_only_when_explicit --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0 -- --exact
red.observed: failed before implementation because BenchClients had no from_value helper or sync candidate field

green.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_sync_green cargo test -p ylong_http_client sync_impl:: --no-default-features --features sync,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0
green.observed: 8 sync_impl tests passed, including sync pool capacity preservation and sync connect_tcp TCP_NODELAY tests

green.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_sync_bench_green cargo test -p ylong_http_client --bin https_proxy_bench ut_bench_clients_selects_sync_candidate_only_when_explicit --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0

build_verification.observed: passed; stable rustfmt reported existing warnings for unstable rustfmt.toml options

benchmark.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client sync --scenario all --requests 200,1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_sync_candidate_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/sync-candidate --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/sync-candidate

verdict: The sync public client path is useful diagnostic evidence and materially improves CPU/request geomean to 0.738x of libcurl, but it is still a SOTA counterexample: throughput geomean is 1.024x, worst throughput is 0.718x, p50 max ratio is 1.397x, p95 max ratio is 1.068x, CPU has a failing cell at 1.024x, and RSS remains higher at up to 1.066x. This rules out async-runtime scheduling cost alone as the SOTA-scale lever under the fixed hard thresholds.
